channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Boston Celtics
channel.description: Boston Celtics trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: 365 days 🙏🏽
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Jayson Tatum: 365 days 🙏🏽 x.com

item.title: Front office maintaining asking price of "a young …
item.description: Underdog NBA: Latest on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade front, per @ShamsCharania : - Front office maintaining asking price of "a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks" - Bucks also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Giannis to stay once again - Wolves, Cavs, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers pursued Giannis at the trade deadline

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Largest point differential during a seven-game win …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Largest point differential during a seven-game win streak in the playoffs in NBA history: 1. 2026 Knicks: +185 . . . . . . . . . . . . 2. 1985 Lakers: +133 3. 2017 Cavs: +131 4. 2010 Magic: +129 5. 2026 Thunder: +128 6. 1986 Celtics: +127

item.title: Tracy McGrady says on NBC that his comments about …
item.description: Ben Rohrbach: Tracy McGrady says on NBC that his comments about Jaylen Brown’s “frustrations” with the Celtics were made as an analyst, not based on any conversation he’s had with Brown since the end of the season.

item.title: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in …
item.description: ‪NBA‬: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in the play-by-play era (1997-98) to record 300+ fourth quarter PTS in the postseason before turning 25! Joining: Kobe Bryant (625 PTS) LeBron James (493 PTS) Jayson Tatum (388 PTS) Kevin Durant (388 PTS) Dwyane Wade (357 PTS)

item.title: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with …
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with multiple 30-point series-clinching wins in a single postseason. They join the 2025 Thunder, 2008 Celtics, and 1987 Lakers. All 3 of those teams went on to win the NBA title.

item.title: Front office maintaining asking price of "a young …
item.description: Underdog NBA: Latest on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade front, per @ShamsCharania : - Front office maintaining asking price of "a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks" - Bucks also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Giannis to stay once again - Wolves, Cavs, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers pursued Giannis at the trade deadline

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Largest point differential during a seven-game win …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Largest point differential during a seven-game win streak in the playoffs in NBA history: 1. 2026 Knicks: +185 . . . . . . . . . . . . 2. 1985 Lakers: +133 3. 2017 Cavs: +131 4. 2010 Magic: +129 5. 2026 Thunder: +128 6. 1986 Celtics: +127

item.title: Tracy McGrady says on NBC that his comments about …
item.description: Ben Rohrbach: Tracy McGrady says on NBC that his comments about Jaylen Brown’s “frustrations” with the Celtics were made as an analyst, not based on any conversation he’s had with Brown since the end of the season.

item.title: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in …
item.description: ‪NBA‬: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in the play-by-play era (1997-98) to record 300+ fourth quarter PTS in the postseason before turning 25! Joining: Kobe Bryant (625 PTS) LeBron James (493 PTS) Jayson Tatum (388 PTS) Kevin Durant (388 PTS) Dwyane Wade (357 PTS)

item.title: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with …
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with multiple 30-point series-clinching wins in a single postseason. They join the 2025 Thunder, 2008 Celtics, and 1987 Lakers. All 3 of those teams went on to win the NBA title.

item.title: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the days of Tim Duncan, but the Victor Wembanyama era promises to be the opposite. Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff Game 4 averaged a combined 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics, trailing only Sixers-Celtics Game 7 on NBC following the Kentucky Derby (10.99M) as the most-watched game of the ongoing NBA Playoffs.

item.title: Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the days of Tim Duncan, but the Victor Wembanyama era promises to be the opposite. Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff Game 4 averaged a combined 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics, trailing only Sixers-Celtics Game 7 on NBC following the Kentucky Derby (10.99M) as the most-watched game of the ongoing NBA Playoffs. The last non-clinching early round game to average as large an audience was Warriors-Lakers Game

item.title: Celtics not expected to make major push for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Jake Fischer on Giannis Antetokounmpo: ‘The Celtics have come out and said that they love Jaylen Brown and that they feel good about him. I do know that Boston made a call to the Bucks before February's trade deadline, but I do not think Boston ever loomed as a very serious suitor, and until I hear otherwise, I don't think they're necessarily going to be either.’

item.title: “I’ve worked out with Memphis and Boston. ... Great …
item.description: Scott Richey: “I’ve worked out with Memphis and Boston. ... Great workouts. Great feedback. I think it's all about evaluating my options and seeing what's the best fit." — @IlliniMBB guard Andrej Stojakovic (@AndrejSto2) on his pre-draft process so far. #Illini #NBADraft

item.title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics fan support led to Brad Stevens putting him back in the starting lineup over Gordon Hayward
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Jaylen Brown said he used fan support years ago to start again despite the fact that Brad Stevens preferred Gordon Hayward: “Once you get the people talking about it, the front office is going to start making decisions.” Celtics fans even started a petition: “That eventually made the Celtics put me back into the starting lineup.”

item.title: “I’ve never had a losing season. You’re asking me …
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Jaylen Brown discussed different solutions to tanking on “State of the Game” with @EnjoyBBall before saying he’s strongly against the practice: “I’ve never had a losing season. You’re asking me about tanking: I don’t see it in my future…I don’t want to be a part of it.”

item.title: Jaylen Brown: Maybe at some point, you make that decision that it's not going to work out here, maybe I need to go somewhere else
item.description: Jaylen Brown: We all have different views of ourselves, different views from our parents, different views from people back home, different views from the organization, and it all might not be the reality of where you’re at right now. So the problem, I think, sometimes is that patience and a plan or a strategy doesn’t come into effect. It just thinks, ‘I’m supposed to get this right now.’ And at some point, maybe you make that decision where it’s not going to work out here. Maybe I need to go som

item.title: Jaylen Brown: To me, the biggest misconception is that sports and politics don’t coexist
item.description: “What’s the biggest misconception from a fan about being an NBA player?” Jaylen Brown: “To me, the biggest misconception is that sports and politics don’t coexist. I think most people — I think the average mind thinks, ‘Oh, it’s just sports. It’s not connected to anything. It’s just entertainment.’ So untrue. So untrue. I’ve sat in a million meetings. We’re not supposed to discuss the discretion of what those meetings are, or their topics of conversation, but I will say the partners of our leagu

item.title: Jaylen Brown: Sometimes I wake up feeling like I’m a pawn meant to divert the attention from what’s actually going on
item.description: Jaylen Brown: “Sports is extremely political, whether people realize it or not. Our game, in a sense, is a means of entertainment. And it almost, at times, serves as a distraction from the other stuff that’s going on around in our country. Sometimes I wake up feeling like I’m a pawn, because I’m just a means to divert the attention from what’s actually going on. There are conversations that we’re not having that we should be having right now. But it’s the Super Bowl, it’s All-Star Game. We’re us

item.title: Danny Ainge asked Jaylen Brown: How can you say that sports are a mechanism for control?
item.description: Jaylen Brown: I was in Boston, and I’m thinking I’m talking to a normal beat writer. It happened to be this big writer for The Guardian. And this dude put me on the cover of The Guardian newspaper saying, ‘Jaylen Brown says sports is a mechanism for control.’ That’s when I was 19 years old. Then that went viral. That’s when I got invited to Harvard to go speak about it. So Harvard invited me, based on that article in The Guardian, to come speak about it. That’s why I became the youngest Harvard

item.title: And that was tough to hear, because it’s like, I was …
item.description: Jayson Tatum: Yeah. Trust me, I’ve asked a thousand people a hundred different questions about how to prevent it, why does it happen? And every specialist I’ve talked to would say they’d be lying if they could tell you exactly why it happens. I remember the very next day, I got my MRI, and I asked the doctor, “If I got an MRI the day before, would you have been able to tell me that I was at risk?” And he was like, “Honestly, you might not want to hear this, but your tendon looks perfectly health

item.title: And I was like, “This is some s***.” It was a terrible …
item.description: Jayson Tatum: I remember vividly. I was in Boston that day, and I was doing my routine. I couldn’t put weight on it, so I would have my walker to get to the shower. I would sit down, have to get undressed. I would have to put this plastic thing over my leg, walk to the shower, and sit on the shower seat. It would literally take me like 45 minutes to shower and get dressed. Then I would get on my scooter down the hall, and I would need help to get down the steps. I remember it was raining. It was

item.title: Jayson Tatum: 'I am the definition of what it means to be a mama’s boy'
item.description: Jayson Tatum: Me and my mom are best friends. She was 19 when she had me, and I grew up in a single-parent household my entire life. Seeing my mom struggle to raise me and put food on the table while going to school, while working — she is the most important person that I’m thankful for. I am who I am, and my life turned out the way it did, because of her. When I got drafted, my mom moved to Boston. We lived in the same apartment building, down the hall. We live on the same street now. I am the

item.title: He was born in December. He was literally born 12 …
item.description: Jayson Tatum: Yeah. Having two kids — I was 19 when Deuce was born, and that was the best thing that could have ever happened to me. Craig Melvin: At 19? Jayson Tatum: At 19. Craig Melvin: How so? Jayson Tatum: It wasn’t planned, and it was tough because I was afraid. I was just leaving Duke. I was entering my new career. I was moving to Boston, starting my new life, and now I was about to be a father. He was born in December. He was literally born 12 games into my NBA career. I was nervous. I d

item.title: One thing I’ve been thinking about is skydiving
item.description: Jaylen Brown: One thing I’ve been thinking about is skydiving. I would love to go skydiving over the pyramids in Egypt. I’m afraid of heights. Terrified.

item.title: When you retire, do you think you’ll become a …
item.description: You’re a great example. And you’ve given lectures, y’all, at Harvard. Isn’t that something? And at MIT. So, when you retire, do you think you’ll become a professor? Jaylen Brown: Maybe, perhaps. I don’t know. We’ll see. I feel like I love to teach. It’s kind of in my family line. My mom teaches, and my grandmother did, too.

item.title: Celtics hire former star point guard Isaiah Thomas as pro and college scout
item.description: Isaiah Thomas, the fearless 5-foot-9-inch point guard who captivated fans at TD Garden and became one of the most beloved Celtics of this century, has rejoined the organization that helped turn him into a superstar. According to league sources, Thomas has been hired as a pro and college scout for the Celtics. He will be based in the Seattle area, his hometown, giving president of basketball operations Brad Stevens reinforcements on the West Coast. The team’s regional scouting staff includes form

item.title: Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. the finalists for Social Justice Champion award
item.description: NBA: As we honor the sixth year of the NBA Social Justice Champion award, join us in recognizing Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. as the finalists for the 2025-26 season. This award honors NBA Hall of Famer Kareem Abdul-Jabbar and his lifetime commitment to driving impactful change around social justice.

item.title: Detroit has won 4 elimination games this postseason, …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Detroit has won 4 elimination games this postseason, the most by a team in one playoff run since the Celtics in 2023. They have a +16.0 average margin of victory in elimination games this postseason, 2nd-highest in NBA history (min. 3 games).

item.title: “They’ve all helped me in different ways and all their …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Donte DiVincenzo says he’s spoken to Jayson Tatum, Tyrese Haliburton and Damian Lillard about the process of coming back from an Achilles. “They’ve all helped me in different ways and all their journeys are different.”

item.title: A global media panel of 100 voters selected the winner …
item.description: NBA Communications: A global media panel of 100 voters selected the winner of the 2025-26 Kia NBA Most Valuable Player Award. The complete voting results:

item.title: "I'm not sure why the NBA decided they needed to …
item.description: Gio Rivera: Jaylen Brown on Twitch, responds to NBA Social Justice Champion nomination: "I'm not sure why the NBA decided they needed to create this award. They've actually asked for my participation over the course of the last five or seven years, and I turn them down every time. I don't really feel like you need to be rewarded for your responsibility. I honestly feel like it's a responsibility to my community. I know some people don't feel like that." "Doing something nice for somebody don't a

item.title: "One of the things that we've got to figure out is how …
item.description: Among the reasons that Boston continues to be mentioned as a possible Giannis Antetokounmpo trade suitor is this recent declaration from the aforementioned Stevens: "One of the things that we've got to figure out is how to have more of an impact at the rim. And I think we do need to add to our team to do that."

item.title: Jaylen Brown takes a shot at Stephen A. Smith
item.description: On Sunday night, Jaylen Brown took to his FCHWPO Twitch channel to address Smith directly over comments the $40 million talking head made about Brown’s post-Game 7 livestream. And it only took three words to get a feel for where Brown’s response was heading. “F*ck Stephen A,” Brown said during his Twitch stream Sunday night. Smith spoke critically of Brown on ESPN’s “First Take” after Boston surrendered a 3-1 first-round series lead to the Philadelphia 76ers, primarily focusing on the 2024 NBA c

item.title: “What type of journalism is this?… F*CK Stephen A… My …
item.description: Slime🐍: Celtics star Jaylen Brown goes OFF on Stephen A Smith for questioning why Jayson Tatum wasn’t on his stream and making his own narratives about why the 2025-2026 Celtics season was JB’s favorite 😳👀 “What type of journalism is this?… F*CK Stephen A… My offer still wants. You want me to be quiet and stop streaming? I want you to be quiet and get off these networks. You’re using your platform to do clickbait.”

item.title: If there were a direct correlation between …
item.description: If there were a direct correlation between organizational success and fan passion, then it would come as no surprise that the Celtics, who have an NBA-best 18 titles in their storied history, topped this list with their TD Garden experience. But consider this much as a counter to that logic: The Lakers, their longtime rivals who are second in league history with 17 titles and also boast a massive, worldwide fanbase, received just two votes out of 161 for Crypto.com Arena. That speaks volumes abo

item.title: There’s a fine line between fans being deemed …
item.description: There’s a fine line between fans being deemed passionate vs. obnoxious, and the Celtics’ loyalists are apparently walking it quite well. The former race wasn’t nearly as close as the latter, though, with the TD Garden crowd winning this one going away. As for the Philly fans being pegged as the over-the-top type, it’s hard to blame them for taking out all these decades of frustration on visiting teams. The Sixers haven’t won a title since 1983, and haven’t been to a Finals since the Allen Iverso

item.title: 2020 draft pick Yam Madar to join LSU
item.description: LSU has reportedly agreed to a $5 million deal with Israeli forward Yam Madar, who is a member of the Hapoel Tel Aviv program in Israel. Madar, who’s set to turn 26 in December, was drafted with the 47th pick in the 2020 NBA Draft by the Boston Celtics, but never signed with the team. The 6-foot-3 guard from Israel has been playing professional basketball since 2018 and was named the 2023 EuroLeague Rising Star.

item.title: Jaylen Brown to Stephen A Smith: 'It ain't your job to be a clown'
item.description: Jaylen Brown: "But the fact is that as an athlete, I'm obligated to speak to the media. It's my job. Like he thinks the media is his job, it's my job too. It's in my contract. If I do not speak to the media, then I will be fined. So when they use that comparison where it's my job, it's your job to play basketball. It's also my job to speak to the media. So me, my thought process, how do I work around that? I'm going to do it on my terms. If I got to talk to y'all, I'm going to do it on my terms.

item.title: He maybe doesn't understand that because maybe he's …
item.description: Jaylen Brown on Stephen A. Smith: "This is a narrative that he's creating. This is in journalism. This is him making his own opinion and formulizing it about what I had to say on his platform. And this is why respectfully a lot of people say f**k Stephen A. because this is the type of stuff he does and then he doesn't recognize it. But he's creating a narrative saying that the reason why I'm saying that I had my favorite season is because selfishly I had a best performing year." "Not the fact th

item.title: Celtics in no rush to give Jaylen Brown extension?
item.description: Brad Stevens is no stranger to extensions but giving out that type of money to a 33-year-old is far from a no brainer. Adding that type of long-term commitment is more likely to hurt than help Brown’s trade value in the present even after his standout regular season in 2025-26. Quite simply, there’s no sense of urgency here from a Celtics standpoint to do an extension now beyond just appeasing Brown. That’s not going to be the impetus for Stevens’ next big deal. He’s got bigger decisions to make

item.title: There were some conversations on concepts, but the …
item.description: Shams Charania: "Minnesota, the Lakers, the Celtics, the Heat, the Knicks, those are all the teams that had interest in Giannis at the deadline. There were conversations, some teams more than other teams. You think about teams like Miami and Minnesota, those were more extensive conversations. Those were more back-and-forth engagement conversations. Then you have teams like Cleveland and the Lakers and the Celtics that checked and the Knicks that checked in, that had interest. There were some con

item.title: “She twisted my arm,” Brown said on stream. “Let them …
item.description: Brown revealed his mother convinced him that accepting the honor could create more visibility for the causes and initiatives he supports off the court. “She twisted my arm,” Brown said on stream. “Let them nominate you… winning an award can help bring eyes to projects and initiatives that you’re working on.”

item.title: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020
item.description: Tim Reynolds: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020. 6 are no longer with the teams they took to those Finals. 2024: Jason Kidd, Dallas 2023: Michael Malone, Denver 2022: Ime Udoka, Boston 2021: Monty Williams, Phoenix 2021: Mike Budenholzer, Milwaukee 2020: Frank Vogel, Lakers

item.title: Largest fourth quarter comeback playoffs wins in the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Largest fourth quarter comeback playoffs wins in the play-by-play era: Clippers, down 24 vs MEM in 2012 Knicks, down 22 vs CLE tonight Celtics, down 21 vs NJN in 2002 Warriors, down 20 vs NOP in 2015

item.title: Blazers add Tyler Lashbrook and Mike Williams to advanced stage of head coaching search
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line contributor Jake Fischer, furthermore, that two additional previously unreported names have also advanced to those stages: Boston's Tyler Lashbrook and Utah's Mike Williams. Sources say various candidates will fly into Portland this weekend as the process continues. Portland's search has been multilayered to date and thus challenging even for NBA coaching insiders to track, given that some candidates have spoken primarily to Blazers general manager Joe Cronin,

item.title: He is believed to earn an annual salary in the $3 …
item.description: Jeff Van Gundy returned to full-time NBA coaching in 2024-25 with the Clippers after a 16-year stint with ESPN and one season as a coaching consultant with the Boston Celtics. He is believed to earn an annual salary in the $3 million range to serve as Tyronn Lue's top aide with the Clippers — one of the highest figures in the NBA for an assistant — and there is naturally great curiosity regarding how much Dundon would be willing to offer in comparison to lure Van Gundy to the Pacific Northwest g

item.title: Victor Wembanyama headlines 2025-26 All-Defensive Teams
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Defensive First Team: ▪️ Rudy Gobert, @Timberwolves ▪️ Chet Holmgren, @okcthunder ▪️ Ausar Thompson, @DetroitPistons ▪️ Victor Wembanyama, @spurs ▪️ Derrick White, @celtics

item.title: The Utah Jazz will name Shane Fenske as general …
item.description: The Utah Jazz will name Shane Fenske as general manager of their G League affiliate, the Salt Lake City Stars, league sources told HoopsHype. Fenske is also Utah’s assistant general manager. Previously, the Yale graduate worked for the Boston Celtics and Portland Trail Blazers.

item.title: Bill Simmons: Giannis Antetokounmpo is interested in the Celtics but Boston may not feel the same
item.description: The Ringer’s Bill Simmons thinks the Milwaukee Bucks could land on the opposite end of a blockbuster trade with the Celtics this summer. “I think Giannis (Antetokounmpo) wants to go to Boston, and I’m not sure Boston wants Giannis," Simmons stated. "I think that’s the push and pull right now.” “I think he wants to stay in the East. I think a certain guy on the Celtics has the same shooting coach as him. I think there’s a lot of respect for the organization. I just think that would be a team he w

item.title: Jaylen Brown, Kawhi Leonard, Jalen Brunson earn All-NBA Second Team honors
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia All-NBA Second Team: Jaylen Brown, @celtics Jalen Brunson, @nyknicks Kevin Durant, @HoustonRockets Kawhi Leonard, @LAClippers Donovan Mitchell, @cavs

item.title: Jaylen Brown shouts out Derrick White for making the …
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Jaylen Brown shouts out Derrick White for making the All-Defensive 1st Team: “Had a hell of a defensive season” He went on to say he’s jealous of White’s defensive timing: “His timing & ability to make impact plays…he’s gifted at that…His IQ sets himself apart.” @FCHWPO

item.title: “I’m not surprised. I’m aware of certain things…who I …
item.description: Bobby Manning: Jaylen on making All-NBA 2nd Team: “I’m not surprised. I’m aware of certain things…who I am, how I use my platform. I’m not the most liked player in the media, at times. At times I’m not the most liked by the fans … some of the things I say and do are a little controversial…”

item.title: Jayson Tatum showing Jaylen Brown some love for making …
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Jayson Tatum showing Jaylen Brown some love for making All-NBA:

item.title: 27% of players with a variation of the name "Jalen" …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: 27% of players with a variation of the name "Jalen" made an All-NBA team this year. 15 players named Jalen/Jaylen/Jaylin played during the regular season, 4 were named All-NBA.

item.title: Robert Williams picking up buzz as a free agent
item.description: Now, Wemby's a freak, I get it. He's a once-in-a-lifetime sort of talent. But, is there anybody out there that the Lakers could sign and go, "At least now you've got a competitive big man out there." Bobby Marks: "The one name that picked up the most buzz, a lot of teams in Chicago last week, was Robert Williams. That's the name. You have to be cautious with that because of his games availability, the minutes. That's the one guy, I don't want to say neutralized Victor Wembanyama, but I think gua

item.title: Jaylen Brown: 'Stephen A. is the face of the industry and his willingness to serve his higher ups is a major cause for concern'
item.description: Jaylen Brown: "Listen, I'm not even really focused on Stephen A. I'm really focused on the industry that he represents. Obviously I have my quarrels with Stephen A, but I also have my quarrels with the industry in itself. Stephen A is the face of the industry and his willingness to serve his higher ups is a major cause for concern, and it's caused frustration, but it ain't even really about him. So here's my proposal and after this that I'm done."

item.title: "Stephen A, here's my proposal. Why don't we have a …
item.description: Jaylen Brown: "Stephen A, here's my proposal. Why don't we have a live audience debate, traditional media versus us athletes? And let's do a live debate in front of a live streamed audience at a mutual location like a Harvard or MIT and let's talk about it. Let's see who come out on top. Should be easy. Should be a piece of cake. Mainstream traditional media versus the athlete. Let's set it up. Harvard, MIT, live and let's see who come out on top. You know where to find me. We know where to cont

item.title: "Second team All-NBA. I’m grateful. God is the …
item.description: In his latest Twitch livestream, the Boston Celtics superstar even expressed gratitude and appreciation for earning a spot on the second team of All-NBA selections. "I’m not the most liked fan or media figure. Sometimes I use my platform in a controversial way, so I’m surprised I’m on any team at all, let alone first or second," he said. "Second team All-NBA. I’m grateful. God is the greatest. ... I’m grateful for everything. I’m not surprised about anything. I know who I am, so I get it. I’m su

item.title: Isiah Thomas on the NBA's playoff format change: 'They didn't want the Lakers going out early'
item.description: "Have you seen anything resembling Wembanyama before?" Isiah Thomas: "I would say early early Ralph Sampson, his first three years in the NBA. I believe he was in the NBA Finals his third year, third or fourth year, he was in the NBA Finals playing against the Celtics. Upset the Laker dynasty. Remember they swept the Lakers. And that's when they changed the playoff format because they didn't want the Lakers going out early. So, right? They changed the playoff format. You know, you've been around

item.title: Hawks, Rockets and Blazers interested in Jaylen Brown
item.description: There is a belief in some corners of the league that Atlanta, Houston and Portland all have legitimate trade interest in Jaylen Brown. Which is something to file away if you're looking for participants that might be interested in joining an eventual multi-team Antetokounmpo trade construction.

item.title: Miami and Boston are increasingly perceived to be the …
item.description: I've heard something similar from a few different teams since the Chicago draft combine: Miami and Boston are increasingly perceived to be the potential trade destinations that currently — underline currently — most intrigue Milwaukee's Antetokounmpo.

item.title: The Celtics indeed checked in with Milwaukee on …
item.description: Mutual interest between the Heat and Antetokounmpo isn't exactly headline-worthy. It's an open secret that Miami covets Giannis and, at the very least, boasts an array of young players (Kel'el Ware, Tyler Herro and Jaime Jaquez Jr.) and draft picks that should lead to substantive trade discussions with the Bucks. Meanwhile ... It is unclear, as Simmons noted during that podcast alongside Rob Mahoney, how much reciprocal Giannis interest Boston holds at this juncture. The Celtics indeed checked i

item.title: Paul Pierce says Jaylen Brown should have been first …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Paul Pierce says Jaylen Brown should have been first team All-NBA over Luka Doncic: “I know that’s saying a lot because Luka did lead the league in scoring, but he had more to work with, too. Think about it. He had LeBron all year. You got Reaves, you know, a lot of most of the year. Jaylen Brown, he did the most with the least out of all these guys that’s on this and maybe Cade Cunningham. I mean, you know, but Jaylen Brown didn’t have a second All-Star. A lot of these guys did.

item.title: Jaylen Brown on his offseason plans: “I will continue …
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Jaylen Brown on his offseason plans: “I will continue to get better...I'm coming into my prime. My mind, my body, my athleticism are all coming together. I'm excited to see what I can do and where I can go with it. I don't think I've reached my limit yet.” (@FCHWPO on Twitch)

item.title: For the fourth year in a row, the Eastern Conference …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: For the fourth year in a row, the Eastern Conference champion lost in the ECF the year before: 2026: Knicks (lost in '25 ECF) 2025: Pacers (lost in '24 ECF) 2024: Celtics (lost in '23 ECF) 2023: Heat (lost in '22 ECF) (via UnderdogNBA) #NBA

item.title: If the Cleveland Cavaliers want a shred of hope, the …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: If the Cleveland Cavaliers want a shred of hope, the Knicks are gonna be the fourth straight Eastern Conference finals loser to make the Finals the next year. They follow the Pacers, Celtics and Heat.

item.title: I am grateful for every member of the Celtics …
item.description: Jay King: Joe Mazzulla reiterated in his statement about winning Coach of the Year that he believes the award should be named Staff of the Year. “Thank you to the Lord for the platform he has given me, and to my wife and family who support me on this journey,” said Mazzulla. “Thank you to our players who compete and give it everything they have each night. I am grateful for every member of the Celtics organization whose dedication impacts winning every day. This award belongs to our staff, who a

item.title: Joe Mazzulla wins Coach of the Year
item.description: Shams Charania: Boston Celtics' Joe Mazzulla has won the 2025-26 NBA Coach of the Year award.

item.title: Joe Mazzulla is the first coach of the Boston Celtics …
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Joe Mazzulla is the first coach of the Boston Celtics to win Coach of the Year since Bill Fitch in 1980. He joins Fitch, Tommy Heinsohn, and Red Auerbach as the only Celtics coaches to ever win the award. (P.S. Bill Russell should’ve been COTY as a player-coach)

item.title: Joe Mazzulla believes his Coach of the Year award …
item.description: NBA on NBC and Peacock: Joe Mazzulla believes his Coach of the Year award should go to the entire Celtics coaching staff.

item.title: Luka Garza, Ron Harper Jr., and Jayson Tatum (among …
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Luka Garza, Ron Harper Jr., and Jayson Tatum (among other Celtics) congratulate Joe Mazzulla for winning Coach of the Year on Instagram:

item.title: Donte DiVincenzo to return during the 2026-27 season?
item.description: It is far too early in the recovery process to predict when Donte DiVincenzo will return to action. Tearing an Achilles tendon is among the most serious injuries in the game, often keeping players out for a year or more. Boston’s Jayson Tatum returned in less than 10 months, but that is considered an outlier. Still, DiVincenzo is trying to follow Tatum’s lead. He flew to New York for surgery less than 24 hours after suffering the injury, following a similar timeline that Tatum went through. DiVi

item.title: Guess who's one of the players Stephen A. Smith has …
item.description: HoopsHype: Guess who's one of the players Stephen A. Smith has supported the most with his NBA award votes through the years? Yes, JAYLEN BROWN. Not a big Giannis guy, though.
